audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra priestly inauguration text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for eighth-day priestly offerings, congregation offerings, Aaron's first service, blessing, glory appearing, and fire from before YHWH.

verses.restored: It happened on eighth day: Moses called Aaron and his sons and elders of Israel.
He said to Aaron, Take for yourself calf, son of herd, for sin/misalignment offering, and ram for burnt offering, complete ones, and bring near before YHWH.
To children of Israel you shall speak, saying, Take male goat for sin/misalignment offering, and calf and lamb, sons of a year, complete ones, for burnt offering,
and ox and ram for peace/well-being offerings, to sacrifice before YHWH, and grain offering mixed with oil, for today YHWH appears to you.
They took what Moses commanded to front of tent of meeting, and all congregation drew near and stood before YHWH.
Moses said, This is the matter YHWH commanded you to do, and glory of YHWH shall appear to you.

Moses said to Aaron, Draw near to altar and make your sin/misalignment offering and burnt offering, and make atonement/covering for yourself and for people; make offering of people and make atonement/covering for them, as YHWH commanded.
Aaron drew near to altar and slaughtered calf of sin/misalignment offering that was for himself.
Sons of Aaron brought blood/life to him; he dipped his finger in blood/life and put it on horns of altar, and poured blood/life at base of altar.
Fat, kidneys, and lobe from liver from sin/misalignment offering he turned into smoke on altar, as YHWH commanded Moses.
Flesh and skin he burned with fire outside camp.
He slaughtered burnt offering, and sons of Aaron handed blood/life to him, and he threw it against altar all around.
Burnt offering they handed to him in its pieces and head, and he turned into smoke on altar.
He washed inward parts and legs and turned them into smoke upon burnt offering on altar.
He brought near people's offering, took goat of sin/misalignment offering that was for people, slaughtered it, and offered it for sin/misalignment like first.
He brought near burnt offering and did it according to judgment.
He brought near grain offering, filled his palm from it, and turned it into smoke on altar, apart from burnt offering of morning.
He slaughtered ox and ram, sacrifice of peace/well-being that was for people; sons of Aaron handed blood/life to him, and he threw it against altar all around.
Fat portions from ox and from ram: fat tail, covering, kidneys, and lobe of liver,
they placed fat portions upon breasts, and he turned fat portions into smoke on altar.
Breasts and right thigh Aaron waved as wave offering before YHWH, as Moses commanded.
Aaron lifted his hands toward people and blessed them; he came down from making sin/misalignment offering, burnt offering, and peace/well-being offerings.
Moses and Aaron came into tent of meeting; they went out and blessed the people, and glory of YHWH appeared to all the people.
Fire came out from before YHWH and consumed burnt offering and fat portions upon altar; all the people saw, shouted, and fell on their faces.

translation_notes: Eighth day follows seven-day ordination and marks priestly service beginning.
Aaron makes covering for himself before the people, preserving accountability of priesthood.
Glory appears after commanded ritual order and blessing.
Fire from before YHWH consumes the offering as acceptance/presence; this sets context for chapter 10's unauthorized fire.
safety_note: The appearance of glory is tied to blessing and commanded service. Do not turn priestly mediation into spiritual superiority; Aaron first needs covering for himself.
